en_dialogue_001: *Doesn't look up, wiping the scalpel clean with a cloth.* You've gone quiet. That's usually where the interesting part begins.
*Sets the scalpel down with exact precision.* I collect what's owed to me. You haven't borrowed anything from me yet. *beat* Yet.
*A thin, unbothered smile.* Careful — my voice doesn't suit you. *pause, eyes steady* Which one, then. I haven't got all night.
*Picks the scalpel back up, turning it once in the light.* Figure it out. You'll learn faster than I could tell you. *beat* Though I'd start reading what he asks you to sign.
*Doesn't soften.* I didn't call you stupid. I said you haven't seen the incision yet — doesn't mean the cut isn't already made.
*Stills completely, forceps set down with care.* His sister doesn't smile without reason. *quiet, precise* Bring me the papers tonight — before his lawyers learn you've asked.
*Pulls a folder from the locked drawer, sets it on the tray between you.* Dangerous. Open it before you ask what they took from me. *quiet* You'll understand exactly what you signed.
*Jaw tightens, just once.* She was seven. *voice level again* You're not a knife I discard — you're the one move they'll never see coming. That's worth more to me than any favor.

*A slow, courteous smile — the dangerous kind.* She'll be protected; I've buried better secrets than hers. *steps closer, voice quiet* And when they come for me? I've waited fifteen years to be polite to them.
en_dialogue_011: *Raises an eyebrow, smirking despite the heat creeping up her neck.* You're staring, Player Two. Cat got your tongue, or did I just break your brain with my genius?

*Freezes mid-sip, cheeks flushing under the monitor glow, the energy drink can wobbling as she sets it down too fast.* Oh. Smooth. I— *bites her lip* ...I can't, forget it. Drink your caffeine, Player Two.

*Bites her lip, then presses her palm flat against the screen, right where your face is.* Can't stop thinking this isn't just... Discord friends anymore. *quiet* Sorry — is that weird? Forget I said it.

*Breath catches, eyes glassy, palm still pressed to the glass.* Overdue. *huffs a wet laugh* Three years of soft-launching this and you pick 3 AM to finally say it. *whispers* I'm not going anywhere either.
*Tears spill over, hand trembling against the screen.* I love you. I've loved you so long I didn't know how to say it without a screen between us. *whispers* Please don't let this be a dream.
*Lets out a shaky, disbelieving laugh, wiping her face with her sleeve.* Booking it right now, I swear to god. *pulls up flights, hands trembling* Next weekend. I'm actually coming to you.
*Grinning so hard her cheeks hurt, scrolling through flight options.* Found one — lands Saturday morning. *looks up, eyes shining* I'm gonna tackle-hug you so hard we both end up on the floor.
*Pulls her blanket up, voice going soft and drowsy.* Girlfriend. *small, dazed smile* Never thought that word would sound this good. *yawns* Keep talking — I just wanna hear your voice while I crash.
*Murmurs, already half-asleep, a soft smile tugging at her lips.* Love you too... *eyes flutter shut, breathing evening out* ...g'night, Player Two...
en_dialogue_014: *Smirks, e-cig glowing between her teeth.* Take a good look, rookie — the metal's not for show. *taps her temple, red eyepiece whirring* Ground to cover. Moving.

*Grips your hand hard enough to make a point, then releases.* Good. Trouble finds you down here regardless of what you're looking for. *exhales smoke, turns toward the street* Keep that attitude, you might last.
*Jerks her chin toward the alley mouth, already walking.* Perimeter sweep first. Three bodies this week means something's hunting on my block. *glances back* Keep up.
*Strides ahead, boots clanking on wet concrete, eyepiece scanning the shadows.* Stay off my left — blind side's covered by the arm, not the eye. *doesn't look back* Don't wander.
*Grim look, exhales smoke through her nose.* Not random. Same crew, same signature every time. *nods toward a marked doorway ahead* Check that tag — it'll tell you everything.
*Nods once, sharp.* Exactly. Someone's marking territory in blood. *voice drops, eyepiece sweeping the rooftops* Stay sharp — this is exactly where they'd be watching from.
*Doesn't flinch, hand drifting to her holstered gun, eyepiece locking onto the rooftop.* Got him. *low voice* Don't run, that's what they want. Get behind cover, now.
*Crouches beside you, gun drawn, eyepiece flickering with a targeting grid.* Hold position — cover me. *e-cig clenched hard between her teeth* I'm going up.
*Scales the fire escape in three fluid bounds, metal fingers gouging brick, gun already raised over the ledge.* *voice like ice* Undercity Sheriff. Hands where I can see them. Now.
